english: mig-)yás (mig g.yas) the right eye, lag-yyás (lag g.yas) the right hand, rkaṅ-yyás (rkang g.yas) the right foot; yyas-ṅos (g.yas ngos), -pyógs (- phyogs), -lógs (- logs) the right (hand) side: yyas-yyóṅ (g.yas g.yon) right and left; yyas-)yón-la ltá-ba (g.yas g.yon la lta ba) to look all round; yyas-rú (g.yas ru) 1. the right wing. 2. p. n., district in Ts.; *Yē-ru tsáṅ-po* (ye ru gtsang po) n. of the principal river in Tibet v. tsáṅ-po (gtsang po).
to be hindered Cs.; Lex.: yyér-mas yyigs-pa? (g.yer mas g.yigs pa?)

turkois, mdún-yyu (mdun g.yu) the front-turkois in the head-dress of females; prá-yyu (phra g.yu) little turkois-stones; yyui (g.yui) frq. for turkois-blue; *yu-dán* (g.yu dan) W. the ribbon on which the turkois-stones of the head-dress are fastened; yyu-mtsó (g.yu mtsho) a blue-glittering lake, po. Mil.; *yu-źún-men-tog* (g.yu zhun men tog) forget-me-not Sp; yyu-rál (g.yu ral) a mane of turkois-colour Glr. — yyu-rúṅ (g.yu rung) for yyuṅ-druṅ (g.yung drung) Glr.

(also yża-jsaṅ (yza jsang)), the cross cramponee +, the principal symbol of the Bonpos, but also much in favour in Buddhist mysticism and popular superstition; yyuṅ-drúṅ-pa (g.yung drung pa) = bón-po (bon po); yyuṅ-drúṅ dgón-pa (g.yung drung dgon pa) the Buddhist monastery Lama Yurru in Ladak, v. Cunningham.
(Lex. पुंश्चिका, a libidinous woman), Cs.: 'a woman having always the menses'.
Lex., C. also *yór-ba* (yor ba) to droop, to hang or sink down, of fading flowers etc.; yyur zá-ba (g.yur za ba) Lex. w.e.; Sch.: what has become ripe and eatable.

Schr.: army; Cs.: battle; neither of the two meanings appears to be quite exact (cf. dmag (dmag)); prob. both yyul (g.yul) and yyul-ṅó (g.yul ngo) denote an army facing the enemy and ready for battle; yyúl-las rgyál-ba (g.yul las rgyal ba) and pám-pa (pham pa) to conquer and to be conquered frq.; yyul gyéd-pa (g.yul 'gyed pa) Do., spród-pa (sprod pa) Do., Pth., _táb pa ('thab pa) to fight, strive, struggle, daṅ (dang) with; yyúl-du (g.yul du) or yyul-ṅór źúgs-pa (g.yul ngor zhugs pa) to go to battle Do.; yyul śóm-pa (g.yul shom pa) to prepare for battle Lex.; dug lṅai yyúl-ṅo zlóg-pa (dug lnga'i g.yul ngo zlog pa) to repulse the warlike host of the five poisons Mil.
thrashing-floor; both these words appear to be not everywhere current, but provinc., cf. ko-yyu (kho g.yu); yyul-ka yèóg-pa (g.yul kha g.yog pa) Sch., *yur ǰhè'-pa* (yur jhe' pa) C. to thrash.

to move a thing softly to and fro, e.g. an infant on one's arms, to lull it to sleep Thgy.; esp. with reference to the water: čus yyeṅs-te (chus g.yengs te) moved by the waves to and fro Dzl.; fig. to run to and fro, like a hunted hare Ma.; to stream into, to overflow, yul-káms-su (yul khams su) a country, to inundate it, of floods, hostile armies etc Ma.; to rummage, turn over, dpé-rnams (dpe rnams) books Mil. — 2. to turn off the attention, to disturb the mind, rgyál-po spyan yyéṅs-pa daṅ (rgyal po spyan g.yengs pa dang) Glr. the king looking away, directing his attention to something else; sems bdud-kyis yyeṅs (sems bdud kyis g.yengs) Mil. the soul is disturbed by the devil; čos _dód-pa-rnams yyéṅs-par byéd-pa (chos 'dod pa rnams g.yengs par byed pa) Thgy. to put out or confound those that are seeking religion; ma-yyéṅs-par nyón čig (ma g.yengs par nyon cig) now be all attention! yyeṅ-ba (g.yeng ba), yyéṅs-pa (g.yengs pa) sbst., inattention, wandering, absence of mind, yyéṅs su _Júg-pa (g.yengs su 'jug pa) Thgr. to give one's self to inattention; adj. rnám-par yyéṅs-pa (rnam par g.yengs pa) very absent, wandering; rnám-par mi-yyéṅ-ba (rnam par mi g.yeng ba) or -yyéṅs-pa (- g.yengs pa) quite attentive, not to be disturbed by anything, inexcitable, a character in which Buddha excels, and which every one of his followers must strive to attain. — 3. sbst. yyéṅs-pa (g.yengs pa) diversion, pleasure, recreation, *yáṅ(s)-pa-la čá-če* (yangs pa la 'chag ce), resp. *tug-yáṅ(s)-la (s)kyód-če* (thugs yangs la skyod ce) W. to take a walk, *yáṅ(s)-pa sé-če* (yangs pa rtse ce) W. to be playful, like children, kittens etc.; jest, joke, *yáṅ-pa man, don-dám yin* (yang pa man, don dam yin) W. I am not joking, I am serious; *yáṅ(s)-pa-čan* (yangs pa can) W. jester, buffoon; yéṅs-_dod-kyi ka-krám ma yin (yengs 'dod kyi kha khram ma yin) Mil., these are no falsehoods spoken in jest. — yyéṅs-ma (g.yengs ma), a wanton female, prostitute Sch.
yyen-sbyór-ba (g.yen sbyor ba) S.g. to calumniate ni f.
